title: quercus lanuginosa subsp. palensis
intro: Quercus lanuginosa subsp. palensis, also known as the Japanese scrub oak or Japanese white oak, is a subspecies of deciduous oak tree native to Japan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus lanuginosa subsp. palensis include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the leaves or bark into teas, infusions, or capsules, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
